workflow.name: Monitor [PROD] workflows in real time with the n8n Public API dashboard
workflow.description:
A real-time monitoring dashboard for your n8n production workflows, accessible directly from the browser via webhook.

Tag any workflow with [PROD] and it will automatically appear on the dashboard showing:

→ Healthy vs error workflows
→ Success rate per workflow
→ Total errors and successes
→ Last error timestamp
→ 7-day error history chart

The dashboard auto-refreshes every 30 seconds. No external tools required — it uses only the n8n Public API.


![Captura de imagem_20260224_143620.png](fileId:4552)![Captura de imagem_20260224_143639.png](fileId:4553)

## How it works

When the webhook is triggered, the workflow:
1. Fetches all workflows tagged [PROD] via the n8n API
2. Retrieves the last 50 executions (success + error) for each workflow
3. Calculates metrics and renders a dark-themed HTML dashboard
4. Returns the dashboard directly in the browser

## Requirements

- n8n self-hosted (any version with Public API enabled)
- n8n API credential configured
- At least one workflow tagged [PROD]

## Setup

1. Enable the n8n Public API under Settings → API
2. Create an n8n API credential with your API key
3. Open the Config node and set your n8n base URL
4. Connect the credential to all HTTP Request nodes
5. Activate the workflow and open the webhook URL in your browser

parameters.content:
## 📊 n8n Resiliency Dashboard

This workflow generates a **real-time HTML dashboard** that monitors all your `[PROD]`-tagged workflows.

### What it does
- Lists all workflows tagged `[PROD]`
- Fetches the last 50 success and 50 error executions per workflow
- Calculates success rate, error count and last error timestamp
- Renders an auto-refreshing HTML dashboard (every 30 seconds)
- Serves it via Webhook — just open the URL in your browser

### Setup
1. Enable the n8n API: **Settings → n8n API**
2. Create an **n8n API credential** and connect it to the two HTTP Request nodes
3. Set your instance URL in the **Config** node
4. Tag the workflows you want to monitor with `[PROD]`
5. Activate this workflow and open the Webhook URL

### Webhook URL
`https://YOUR-N8N-URL/webhook/resiliency-dashboard`

### ⚙️ Step 1 — Config
Set your n8n base URL here.

**Example:**
`https://your-instance.n8n.cloud`

This URL is reused by all HTTP Request nodes.

### 🔍 Step 2 — Fetch & Filter
Fetches all workflows via the n8n API, then filters only those tagged `[PROD]`.

To monitor a workflow, add the tag `[PROD]` to it in n8n Settings.

### 📥 Step 3 — Fetch Executions
For each `[PROD]` workflow, fetches the last **50 successes** and **50 errors** in parallel.

Both run simultaneously via branched connections.

### 📊 Step 4 — Compare & Render
Merges success + error executions, computes per-workflow stats (success rate, error count, last error timestamp), builds a 7-day error chart and renders the full HTML dashboard, served via the Webhook response node.
